metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact anchor phrases (or a canonical example for each of the three elements: small-scale default, prohibited-reasoning, two-directional bar) that must appear in A1/T5a so AC1's grep proof is deterministic and unambiguous.
findings.finding: The G-9 anchor text (small-scale default, prohibited-reasoning examples, two-directional bar) that must be added to A1/T5a is not specified in the plan; an executor must invent the exact wording, making AC1's grep proof self-referential and unexecutable as written.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Required phrases' subsection to the What/AC1 block, e.g.: small-scale default phrase: 'A minor defect in a small file is not automatically major'; prohibited-reasoning phrase: 'Do not inflate severity because the topic is important'; two-directional phrase: 'calibration runs both ways: do not suppress a genuine finding any more than you inflate a trivial one'. Then AC1 proof becomes: `grep -c 'A minor defect in a small file' plan_review_A1.md plan_review_T5a.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define at least a brief scenario description for each of the two eval cases (e.g. a snippet of the input plan + expected verdict direction) so the executor and verifier agree on what 'fires' and 'is suppressed' mean.
findings.finding: The two eval.yaml test cases required by AC3 are not specified: the plan says 'a genuine high-scale/high-blast finding still fires; an inflated-scale finding is suppressed' but provides no scenario inputs, expected verdicts, or failure definitions for either case.
findings.location: AC3 / What section — Behavioral proof runs bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact canonical phrases for 'small-scale default', 'prohibited-reasoning examples', and 'two-directional bar' in the plan body (or a linked spec), so the AC grep step has determinate strings to check.
findings.finding: The 'small-scale default', 'prohibited-reasoning examples', and 'two-directional bar' anchor text that must be inserted into plan_review_A1.md and plan_review_T5a.md are referenced by name but never defined anywhere in the plan. An executor must invent the exact wording, making the AC unverifiable ('grep the phrases') since no canonical phrases are specified.
findings.location: What section / AC line 1: 'A1/T5a carry the small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning + two-directional text. Proof: grep the phrases in both files.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Provide the minimum eval-case fields for both new dataset entries in the plan: id, corpus, criterion, expect (finding/pass), mode, and a representative input plan snippet, so the behavioral proof is unambiguously implementable.
findings.finding: The two new eval cases required in plan-review-finder.eval.yaml ('a genuine high-scale/high-blast finding still fires'; 'an inflated-scale finding is suppressed') are referenced by description only — no plan-text inputs, criterion ids, expect labels, mode tags, or corpus fields are defined. The eval-case content is entirely unspecified, leaving the executor to fabricate both inputs from scratch.
findings.location: What section / AC line 3: 'TWO cases added to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml: a genuine high-scale/high-blast finding still fires; an inflated-scale finding is suppressed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one discrimination case to `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml` specifically exercising the blast_radius 'never raises' ratchet (e.g., a finding about a doc-only wording change whose plan's subject is system-wide — the verifier must keep blast_radius=local, not elevate it to system).
findings.finding: The FP-3(b) blast_radius 'never raises' ratchet is added to the Pass-2 verifier prompts (plan_review_verifier.md + plan_review_verifier_agentic.md) and the SeverityAttrs docstring, but the AC specifies behavioral proof only in the Pass-1 finder eval (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml). The verifier has its own eval file (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml) with a 'discriminates_impact_levels' scorer, but no case specifically exercising the new one-way blast_radius ratchet is added there. The new verifier constraint therefore has no targeted behavioral proof.
findings.location: AC line 3 / What section FP-3(b) / plan-review-finder.eval.yaml vs plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml
findings.suggested_fix: In plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml, add a pair: one finding where the flaw is local/cosmetic but the plan's overall subject is system-wide (blast_radius should stay local — tests the 'never raises' ratchet), and confirm the existing discriminates_impact_levels scorer or a new discriminates_blast_radius scorer covers this axis.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Redirect the two scale eval cases from plan-review-finder.eval.yaml to plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml (where severity attributes are scored), and confirm they complement or extend the existing I1-high/I1-low impact-discrimination pair rather than duplicating it.
findings.finding: The two scale eval cases (genuine high-scale fires; inflated-scale suppressed) are directed to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml, but the blast_radius 'never raises' ratchet being added is a Pass-2 VERIFIER behaviour (severity attributes are scored in Pass-2, not Pass-1). The finder (Pass-1) does not score blast_radius at all — it only emits findings. The correct home for scale-calibration behavioral proofs is plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml, which already has impact-discrimination pairs (I1-high / I1-low) that partially cover the same axis.
findings.location: AC3 / What bullet 'Behavioral proof runs ... Add the two scale cases there' / src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml
findings.suggested_fix: Add the two cases to plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml as a new {kind: true, pair: scale-calibration} / {kind: false, mode: inflated-scale, pair: scale-calibration} pair using the discriminates_impact_levels or a new discriminates_blast_radius scorer; reference plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml in AC3's proof command.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the code-review gate as a consumer of SeverityAttrs.blast_radius in the What section, and confirm the 'never raises' wording is semantically correct for code-review findings (or restrict the ratchet to the plan-review verifier prompt text rather than the shared contract field description).
findings.finding: The SeverityAttrs.blast_radius field description in review_kernel/verify.py is shared by ALL gates that import verification_model — including the code-review gate (src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py uses review_kernel.verify_instructions and the same registered 'verification' contract). The plan's AC grep proof enumerates only plan-review verifier consumers, not the code-review pass-2 path, leaving the cross-gate blast_radius doc change unenumerated.
findings.location: What / FP-3(b) bullet / AC2 grep proof / src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py SeverityAttrs.blast_radius

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Write the exact sentence (or a brief paragraph) that encodes the 'blast_radius only LOWERS, never raises' ratchet in the plan body, so all three edit targets receive identical wording.
findings.finding: The 'blast_radius only LOWERS, never raises' ratchet rule is described as a decision protocol to add to both verifier prompts AND the SeverityAttrs.blast_radius doc in verify.py, but the exact sentence/phrasing is never written in the plan. The rule is referenced as a named concept, not as a copyable text, so the co-location requirement (rule alongside the schema that needs it) cannot be satisfied deterministically.
findings.location: What section / AC line 2: 'One-way blast_radius phrasing in both verifier prompts + the SeverityAttrs doc. Proof: grep -l "never raises" ...'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: "Canonical ratchet sentence: 'blast_radius reflects only the FLAW's reach — it may be lower than the feature's reach but NEVER higher; do not inherit system-level blast_radius from a large subject matter.'"

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
